phase_status: VERIFY FINAL FAILED 2026-07-17 at HEAD f12ffba. All six slices S-000..S-005 are applied and committed and the suite is genuinely green (1797 pass / 0 fail in a /private/tmp worktree) — the BUILD is intact; the GATE is what failed. sdd-verify --mode=final returned `fail` (verify-report.md) on a single gating item, F-01. The four BLIND anti-anchored Council personas run in parallel with it (architect, qa-engineer, security-engineer, tech-writer — all opus) found substantially MORE, including four real CORRECTNESS bugs in addImport that the verifier missed. The fan-out is what caught them; verify-final alone called this 'a one-line JSDoc fix, not a re-plan' and would have shipped it. Full evidence: council-findings.md (this folder) + engram obs #2288. ROUTED: Planner -> sdd-spec (unfreeze=true, spec is V4 SIGNED) -> sdd-apply -> re-verify final -> judgment-day. NOTE: this mirror was frozen at a stale `build-complete` for ~1h while a macOS TCC fault (engram obs #2290, recurring) cut off all ~/Documents access before the FAIL could be written; corrected on resume.

QA-5 exists precisely because every probe so far was an absence mutant.
spec_detail: V5 minted 2026-07-17 (unfrozen from V4-SIGNED), AWAITING OWNER SIGN — blocks sdd-apply. AMENDED in place, all 14 V4 REQ-IDs preserved, none removed or reused: REQ-RXD-05 (merge-or-create made shape-aware — a 'local name' definition + merge scoped strictly to non-type-only named clauses; ONE idea closes all four Council correctness bugs; scenarios 05.1-05.4 unchanged in expected outcome, they were the correct baseline), REQ-RXD-06 (a: adds an IMPORT_RESERVED_WORDS denylist of 46 ECMAScript reserved/strict-mode words + await — real behaviour change; b: removes the false 'full prototype-walk set' claim, replaced with the accurate Set-key-safety rationale — wording only), REQ-RXD-11 (11.5's literal example '{' was unimplementable under ADR-03 → corrected to the delimiter-balanced '{1+}' which actually demonstrates the claimed property; closes a long-standing carry-forward). MINTED: REQ-RXD-15 (contributor docs must teach the validatedOp pattern — closes ARCH-1). New scenarios under existing REQs: 05.5-05.10, 06.7, 06.8, 11.6. History: V3 minted+retired 2026-07-16; V4 owner-signed 2026-07-16.

carry_forward: REQ-RXD-11.5 wording amendment -> pending-changes at archive.

Strict TDD ordering violation on S-002 (impl written same editing pass as tests; RED proven retroactively) is DISCLOSED, not resolved — sdd-verify --mode=final MUST audit it in TDD Cycle Adherence. Do not let it downgrade to clean by attrition.

RESOLVED 2026-07-17 by diagnostic — full-suite instability is ENVIRONMENTAL, not a regression and not parallelism. The 'parallel-execution flakiness' hypothesis is REFUTED: bun test is sequential by default and package.json:64 runs bare `bun test` with no flags; nothing was ever parallel. Control experiment: HEAD's exact code (6ee9af0) checked out into /private/tmp gives 1779 pass / 0 fail, 3/3 runs (~23s) — so apply-progress.md's '1779/0' claim is truthful and exactly reproducible, just not under ~/Documents. Mechanism: subprocess-bound tests (bun install / bun link / tsc --noEmit via blocking spawnSync) run against bun's default 5000ms timeout while first-touch file scanning under ~/Documents inflates the first `tsc --noEmit` from 663ms to 16694ms (26x). tsc --extendedDiagnostics proves identical work both sides (256 files / 48335 symbols / 556 types). Strong inference: Microsoft Defender (wdavdaemon) real-time scan; NOT A/B-proven (corporate EDR cannot be disabled to confirm). Ruled out: parallelism, regression, ts-morph/type-graph growth, dist/testing/commons d.ts drift, core AST leakage (FIT-37 holds), iCloud sync, port binding, process.cwd() mutation. NO shared-state defect exists.

OPEN (pre-existing debt, NOT react-dialect scope): subprocess-bound tests declare no explicit timeout — test/docs/quickstart-docs.test.ts:312 and :319, plus the S-03 permissive-proof CI guard and the installed-consumer-vantage e2e leg. Their measured work is ~2s even on a fast filesystem against a 5000ms budget; the declared budget does not describe the work regardless of Defender. Fix = explicit per-test timeouts (~30 min). Do NOT raise the global timeout (masks genuinely slow tests). Environment-level companion fix (not committed): Defender real-time-scan exclusion for the repo or at minimum .tmp-*/ scratch dirs.
